length.description: Returns the number of characters in string; for byte-array values, returns the actual byte length.

match.description: Returns 1 if string matches the glob pattern, or 0 otherwise.

range.description: Returns the inclusive range of characters from first through last.

first.description: Returns the index of the first exact occurrence of needleString in haystackString, or -1 if not found.

last.description: Returns the index of the first character in the last exact occurrence of needleString in haystackString, or -1 if not found.

index.description: Returns the character at charIndex, or an empty string if the index is outside string.

trim.description: Returns string with leading and trailing characters from chars removed, defaulting to whitespace and NUL.

trimleft.description: Returns string with leading characters from chars removed, defaulting to whitespace and NUL.

trimright.description: Returns string with trailing characters from chars removed, defaulting to whitespace and NUL.

tolower.description: Returns string with upper or title case letters converted to lower case, optionally within the first..last range.

toupper.description: Returns string with lower or title case letters converted to upper case, optionally within the first..last range.

totitle.description: Returns string with the first selected character title-cased and the rest of the selected range lower-cased.

equal.description: Returns 1 if string1 and string2 are identical under the selected comparison options, or 0 otherwise.

compare.description: Compares string1 and string2 character by character and returns -1, 0, or 1.

is.description: Returns 1 if string is a valid member of the specified character class, or 0 otherwise.

map.description: Returns string with substrings replaced according to the key-value pairs in mapping.

repeat.description: Returns string concatenated with itself count times.

replace.description: Returns string with the inclusive first..last character range removed or replaced by newstring.
replace.example: string replace "Hello World" 6 end "Tcl"  ;# => Hello Tcl

reverse.description: Returns string with its characters in reverse order.

cat.description: Concatenates the supplied strings directly and returns the resulting string.

wordstart.description: Returns the index of the first character in the word containing charIndex; this subcommand is obsolete.

wordend.description: Returns the index just after the last character in the word containing charIndex; this subcommand is obsolete.

bytelength.description: Returns the byte count of Tcl's internal modified UTF-8 representation of string; this subcommand is obsolete.

join.description: Combines one or more path names using the correct separator for the current platform; an absolute later component discards earlier components.

dirname.description: Returns all path components except the last element; a single relative component returns . and a root path returns the root.

tail.description: Returns the last filesystem component of a path, ignoring any trailing directory separator.

extension.description: Returns the last extension of the final path component, including the dot, or an empty string when there is no extension.

rootname.description: Returns the path up to but not including the last dot in the final component, or the original name when there is no extension.

exists.description: Returns 1 if the path exists and the current user has search privileges for the directories leading to it, otherwise 0.

isfile.description: Returns 1 if the path names a regular file, otherwise 0.

isdirectory.description: Returns 1 if the path names a directory, otherwise 0.

mkdir.description: Creates each named directory and any missing parent directories; existing directories are accepted without error.

copy.description: Copies files or directories; multiple sources are copied into the target directory, and existing targets are overwritten only with -force.

delete.description: Removes each pathname; non-empty directories require -force, and deleting a non-existent pathname is not an error.

rename.description: Renames or moves files and directories; multiple sources are moved into the target directory, and existing targets are overwritten only with -force.

nativename.description: Returns the platform-specific native form of a path, useful when passing a file name to operating-system commands.

normalize.description: Returns an absolute normalized path representation with . and .. removed, using the standard native format for the platform.

stat.description: Stores file status fields in array varName, including atime, ctime, dev, gid, ino, mode, mtime, nlink, size, type, and uid, and returns an empty string.

size.description: Returns the size of the named file in bytes, or raises an error if the size cannot be queried.

readable.description: Returns 1 if the named file is readable by the current user, otherwise 0.

writable.description: Returns 1 if the named file is writable by the current user, otherwise 0.

executable.description: Returns 1 if the named file is executable by the current user, otherwise 0; on Windows, directories and .exe, .com, .cmd, or .bat files are treated as executable.

mtime.description: Returns or sets the last modification time of the file as POSIX epoch seconds.

atime.description: Returns or sets the last access time of the file as POSIX epoch seconds; some filesystems, such as FAT on Windows, do not support access time.

type.description: Returns the file type string: file, directory, characterSpecial, blockSpecial, fifo, link, or socket.

exists.description: Returns 1 if the named variable exists in the current scope, otherwise 0.

commands.description: Returns names of visible commands that match pattern, or all visible commands if pattern is omitted.

procs.description: Returns names of Tcl procedures in the current namespace that match pattern, or all procedures if pattern is omitted.

args.description: Returns the argument names of the named procedure in order.

body.description: Returns the body script of the named procedure.

level.description: Returns the current stack level, or the command and arguments at the specified stack level.

script.description: Returns the innermost script file currently being evaluated, optionally overriding that value for the active invocation.

hostname.description: Returns the host name of the machine for the current Tcl invocation.

patchlevel.description: Returns the exact Tcl patch level stored in tcl_patchLevel.

tclversion.description: Returns the major.minor Tcl version stored in tcl_version.

globals.description: Returns names of currently defined global variables that match pattern, or all global variables if pattern is omitted.

locals.description: Returns names of currently defined local variables that match pattern, excluding variables defined by global, upvar, or variable.

vars.description: Returns names of currently visible variables that match pattern, including locals and visible globals.

default.description: Returns 1 if the procedure argument has a default value and stores it in varname, otherwise returns 0.
default.example: set hasDefault [info default myProc voltage defVal]

exists.description: Returns 1 if arrayName is an array variable, otherwise 0.

get.description: Returns a key-value list of array elements whose names match pattern, or all elements if pattern is omitted.

set.description: Sets array elements from an even-length key-value list, creating the array if needed.

names.description: Returns array element names matching pattern with -exact, -glob, or -regexp matching; the default mode is -glob.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the array, or 0 if arrayName is not an array.

unset.description: Unsets array elements matching pattern, or unsets the whole array if pattern is omitted.

statistics.description: Returns statistics about the hash table distribution used to represent the array.

append.description: Appends strings to the value mapped by key in a dictionary variable, treating a missing key as an empty string.

create.description: Returns a new dictionary from alternating key and value arguments.
create.example: set d [dict create name test value 42]

exists.description: Returns a boolean indicating whether the key path exists in the dictionary value.

filter.description: Returns a new dictionary containing only entries that match a key, value, or script filter.

for.description: Iterates over dictionary entries in insertion order with key and value variables set for each entry.

get.description: Returns the value at the given key path, or a key-value list when no key is provided.

incr.description: Adds an integer increment to the value mapped by key in a dictionary variable, treating a missing key as 0.

info.description: Returns implementation-dependent internal information about the dictionary for display.

keys.description: Returns dictionary keys matching the optional glob pattern, in insertion order.

lappend.description: Appends values to the list mapped by key in a dictionary variable, treating a missing key as an empty list.

map.description: Applies body to each dictionary entry and returns a new dictionary containing the collected results.

merge.description: Returns a dictionary containing all input dictionaries, with later mappings overriding earlier ones.

remove.description: Returns a new dictionary with the listed top-level keys removed; missing keys are ignored.

replace.description: Returns a copy of the dictionary with listed key-value pairs replaced or added.

set.description: Sets the value at a key path in a dictionary variable, creating or updating nested dictionaries as needed.

size.description: Returns the number of key-value mappings in the dictionary value.

unset.description: Removes the mapping at a key path from a dictionary variable and returns the updated dictionary value.

update.signature: dict update dictionaryVariable key varName ?key varName ...? body
update.description: Maps selected dictionary entries to variables while body executes and writes variable changes back afterward.
update.example: dict update opts voltage v {set v [expr {$v * 2}]}

values.description: Returns dictionary values matching the optional glob pattern, in the insertion order of their keys.

with.description: Maps dictionary entries to same-named variables while body executes and writes changes back afterward.

children.description: Returns the fully-qualified names of child namespaces belonging to a namespace, optionally filtered by a glob pattern.

code.description: Captures the current namespace context and returns a scoped script that can be executed later from any namespace.

current.description: Returns the fully-qualified name of the current namespace, using :: for the global namespace.

delete.description: Deletes one or more namespaces together with their variables, procedures, and child namespaces.

ensemble.description: Creates, configures, queries, or tests ensemble commands that dispatch to subcommands.

eval.description: Activates a namespace, creating it if needed, and evaluates code in that namespace context.

exists.description: Returns 1 if namespace is a valid namespace in the current context, otherwise returns 0.

export.description: Sets or queries the export patterns that determine which commands may be imported from the current namespace.
export.example: namespace eval ::demo { namespace export run reset }

forget.description: Removes commands previously imported into the current namespace, undoing matching namespace import operations.

import.description: Imports exported commands into the current namespace, or returns the list of commands already imported when called without patterns.

inscope.description: Executes a script in an existing namespace and appends extra arguments as proper list elements.

origin.description: Returns the fully-qualified original command name behind an imported command.

parent.description: Returns the fully-qualified name of the parent namespace for the specified namespace or for the current namespace.

path.description: Returns or sets the command resolution path of the current namespace.

qualifiers.description: Returns the leading namespace qualifiers from a qualified name without checking whether those namespaces exist.

tail.description: Returns the simple name at the end of a qualified namespace, command, or variable name.

upvar.description: Links local variables in the current procedure to variables in the specified namespace.

unknown.description: Returns or sets the unknown command handler used when command resolution fails inside the current namespace.

which.description: Looks up a command or variable name and returns its fully-qualified name, or an empty string if it is not found.

add.description: Adds one or more time offsets to timeVal and returns the resulting time as epoch seconds.

clicks.description: Returns a system-dependent high-resolution timer value for relative timing measurements.

format.description: Formats a time value expressed as epoch seconds into a date-time string.

microseconds.description: Returns the current time as an integer number of microseconds from the epoch.

milliseconds.description: Returns the current time as an integer number of milliseconds from the epoch.

scan.description: Parses a date-time string and returns the corresponding time as epoch seconds.

seconds.description: Returns the current time as an integer number of seconds from the epoch.

decode.description: Decodes readable encoded data such as base64, hex, or uuencode back into binary data.

encode.description: Encodes binary data as a readable string using the selected binary encoding.

format.description: Packs Tcl values into a binary string according to formatString and returns the result.

scan.description: Parses fields from a binary string, assigns scanned values to variables, and returns the number of conversions performed.
scan.example: binary scan [binary format i 42] i value

convertfrom.description: Converts byte data from the specified encoding into Tcl Unicode characters.

convertto.description: Converts a Tcl Unicode string into byte data in the specified encoding.

dirs.description: Returns or sets the list of directories searched for *.enc encoding data files.

names.description: Returns the names of all encodings currently available to Tcl.

system.description: Returns or sets the system encoding used when Tcl passes strings to system calls.

forget.description: Removes all information about the specified packages from the current interpreter.

ifneeded.description: Returns or registers the script used to make a particular package version available on demand.

names.description: Returns the names of packages that are provided or have package ifneeded scripts in this interpreter.

present.description: Checks that a suitable version of a package is already loaded without trying to load it.

provide.description: Declares that a package version is present, or returns the currently provided version.

require.description: Ensures that a suitable version of a package is loaded and returns the loaded version.

unknown.description: Returns, sets, or clears the fallback command used by package require when no suitable package is found.

vcompare.description: Compares two package version numbers and returns -1, 0, or 1.

versions.description: Returns all versions of a package for which package ifneeded information is available.

vsatisfies.description: Returns 1 if version satisfies at least one version requirement, and 0 otherwise.

prefer.description: Returns or sets whether package require prefers the latest acceptable version or the highest stable acceptable version.

blocked.description: Returns 1 if the last input operation on channelId failed because it would have blocked, otherwise 0.

close.description: Closes channelId, optionally half-closing only the read or write side when direction is supplied; buffered output is flushed as needed.

configure.description: Queries or sets configuration options for channelId, such as blocking mode, buffering, encoding, EOF character, and end-of-line translation.

copy.signature: chan copy inputChan outputChan ?-size size? ?-command callback?
copy.description: Copies data from inputChan to outputChan until EOF or the requested size, optionally running asynchronously with a completion callback.

create.description: Creates a reflected script-level channel using cmdPrefix as its handler command prefix and returns the new channel handle.

eof.description: Returns 1 if the last input operation on channelId reached end-of-file, otherwise 0.

event.description: Gets, installs, or removes a readable or writable file-event handler script for channelId.

flush.description: Forces pending output buffered for channelId to be written, blocking until complete unless the channel is non-blocking.

gets.description: Reads the next line from channelId, returning the line or storing it in varName and returning the character count.

names.description: Returns the names of open channels, optionally filtered with a glob-style pattern.

pending.description: Returns the number of input or output bytes currently buffered internally for channelId, or -1 if that direction is not open.

pipe.description: Creates a standalone pipe and returns a two-element list containing the read-side and write-side channels.

pop.description: Removes the topmost transformation from channelId, or closes the channel if no transformations remain.

postevent.description: Posts reflected-channel events listed in eventSpec to channelId; it is intended for handlers of channels created by chan create.

push.description: Adds a script-level transformation implemented by cmdPrefix on top of channelId and returns the transformation handle.

puts.description: Writes string to channelId followed by a newline unless -nonewline is supplied; stdout is used when channelId is omitted.

read.signature: chan read channelId ?numChars? | chan read ?-nonewline? channelId
read.description: Reads characters from channelId, either up to numChars or until EOF, blocked, or error; -nonewline trims a trailing newline in the all-data form.

seek.description: Sets the current byte offset for channelId relative to start, current, or end and discards buffered input after flushing output.

tell.description: Returns the current byte offset in the underlying stream for channelId, or -1 for channels that do not support seeking.

truncate.description: Sets the underlying stream length for channelId to length, or to the current byte offset when length is omitted, after flushing the channel.
